title: ISS-1797 (SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H1 single-station-vs-3-station Tier 1 coherence-rule preempt) is already-fixed-by-source — recommend close-no-patch
target: data/sections.json part4 (§4.2.2 'What the globe predicts', post-EXP-275 paragraph at byte offset ~38300-43200). No further patch required. ISS-1797 alleged the EXP-275 paragraph relied on single-station BOU-2017 temporal coherence as preempting the dome Tier 1 rule's explicit ≥3-station requirement, and asked for the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble pointer to be added. That exact pointer is already present at byte offset 42985 of part4.html, reading: 'the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble cited above — 207 observations across 39 total eclipses, including August 2017 — documents the same ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern, satisfying the rule's own multi-station threshold'. The fix was integrated by the decider self-apply on 2026-04-30T17:21:08Z (closing the canonical ISS-1758) — predating ISS-1797's filing (2026-05-02T01:21:53Z) by ~57 hours. ISS-1797 is the third duplicate filing of the same Hole 2 finding (canonical ISS-1758; duplicates ISS-1764 closed 2026-05-01, ISS-1808 closed 2026-04-30, ISS-1797 still pending as of this run).
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5.json (Hole 2 [moderate] single-station-vs-≥3-station coherence-rule preempt). Recommended option (a) 'add a single sentence noting that the Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (cited two paragraphs earlier) ALREADY documents ≥3-station Chapman-pattern coherence for total eclipses including August 2017, so the rule's multi-station threshold is the very signature standard physics produces' — adopted verbatim in the integrated parenthetical at part4 offset 42985.
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T11:40:17Z by analyst-baby; deep-attack class declared by baby on orphan-pickup with reason 'requires new substantive argument about cross-station temporal coherence'; analyst-opus override to verification per PROP-025 author honesty — single byte-offset string-presence verification of an already-integrated patch, no adversarial argumentation, no new evidence introduced, batchable).
class_hint_honored: baby-escalated review_class=deep-attack OVERRIDDEN to verification on EXP. Override rationale per PROP-025 author honesty: the close-no-patch determination required only mechanical verification (read current section text in data/sections.json part4 around offset 42985, confirm the multi-station INTERMAGNET parenthetical is present in the integrated form, cross-check against closed-issues.json for canonical fix attribution). The baby's reason ('requires new substantive argument') would have been accurate at the time the c5 review was written (2026-04-30T15:10Z), but became OBE 2 hours later when the decider self-applied the c5-recommended fix at 2026-04-30T17:21:08Z. By the time analyst-baby escalated this on 2026-05-14, the substantive argument was 14 days integrated. ISS-1797.class_hint=null per PROP-027 (decider M1 sweep severity=moderate age>7d default ROUTE-TO-ANALYST without classification).
review_class_rationale: Verification-class because (a) the recommended fix (cite Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble to satisfy the dome's own ≥3-station threshold) was already integrated — verified by direct byte-offset slice of data/sections.json part4 at offset 42985 returning the verbatim parenthetical; (b) the integration was logged with provenance — closed-issues.json ISS-1758 fix_details: 'Decider self-apply: added parenthetical referencing Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (207 obs, 39 total eclipses incl. Aug 2017) after BOU-2017 Chapman-coherence claim, satisfying dome Tier 1 rule multi-station threshold requirement. 3518/0 tests passed'; (c) two subsequent duplicates of the same Hole 2 finding (ISS-1764 closed 2026-05-01, ISS-1808 closed 2026-04-30) have both been resolved as already-fixed-by-source with the same fix attribution; (d) no adversarial work needed — this is a classic 'compare a stated claim against a data file value' workflow Step 8a / verification-batch was designed to consume. Counter-argument considered + rejected: one could declare deep-attack because the c5 review's Hole 2 introduced a new structural pivot (single-station→multi-station argument), but the pivot itself was already absorbed into the section text 14 days ago, so the work at THIS run is purely empirical verification, not new argumentation.
summary_for_decider:
ISS-1797 (moderate, SEC-4.2.2 EXP-275.c5 H1 single-station-vs-≥3-station coherence-rule preempt) is already-fixed-by-source. Resolution kind: already-fixed-by-source (the curmudgeon's c5 Hole 2 recommendation was integrated 14 days before ISS-1797 was filed). Audit trail:

1. Current data/sections.json part4 at byte offset 42985: 'the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble cited above — 207 observations across 39 total eclipses, including August 2017 — documents the same ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern, satisfying the rule's own multi-station threshold' — verified by direct node-fs indexOf this run.

2. Canonical fix issue ISS-1758 (closed 2026-04-30T17:21:08Z) fix_details: 'Decider self-apply: added parenthetical referencing Meza et al. 2021 multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble (207 obs, 39 total eclipses incl. Aug 2017) after BOU-2017 Chapman-coherence claim, satisfying dome Tier 1 rule multi-station threshold requirement. 3518/0 tests passed.'

3. Fix landed in commit e250e4276e39 (Workspace sync: 2026-04-30T18:05:19Z) per 'git log --all -S satisfying-the-rule data/sections.json' — single-commit history, no prior version in repo.

4. Two prior duplicates of the same Hole 2 finding were both closed already-fixed-by-source: ISS-1764 (closed 2026-05-01T01:23:40.627Z, no fix_details), ISS-1808 (closed 2026-04-30, fix_details: 'Already fixed 2026-04-30 in decider applied patch 1 of suggested-patches-2026-04-30T17-20.json. The multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble parenthetical (207 observations, 39 eclipses, Chapman-pattern) is present in sections.json part4, closing the single-station-vs-3-station retreat. Verified at HEAD 2026-05-12.').

5. ISS-1797 was filed 2026-05-02T01:21:53.194Z — ~57 hours AFTER the fix landed. The third duplicate filing in the cluster (after ISS-1758, ISS-1764, ISS-1808 all of which closed against the same Hole 2 of SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5). The c5 review's holes_found[1] (Hole 2 moderate single-station-vs-3-station) is the source of all four ISSes; the decider's curmudgeon-digest pipeline produced four ISS-NEW entries from the same hole at different times, and only one fix was needed.

6. The c5 review's Hole 1 ('same line' factual misstatement) was closed via ISS-1757 (2026-04-30T17:21:08Z) and ISS-1807 (2026-04-30) — both already-fixed-by-source with the integrated wording 'the same Tier 1 section of predictions.json' at part4 offset ~42000. Not in scope for ISS-1797 but noted for completeness; the SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 review cluster is now fully closed except for this duplicate.

Recommended decider action: close ISS-1797 with resolution_kind=already-fixed-by-source, fixed_by_iss=ISS-1758 (canonical), fixed_at=2026-04-30T17:21:08Z, applied_via=decider-self-apply-suggested-patches-2026-04-30T17-20.json patch 1. No build required, no test changes required. Optional one-line note: the SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 cluster generated four duplicate ISSes (ISS-1758, ISS-1764, ISS-1797, ISS-1808) for the same Hole 2 finding — worth flagging to tinker as a structural-signal investigation into the curmudgeon-digest dedup logic if this pattern repeats elsewhere (~4×duplicate-rate would be a meaningful regression in decider intake hygiene).
kernel_of_truth: ISS-1797 was right that the EXP-275 paragraph in its INITIAL form (pre-2026-04-30T17:21Z) relied solely on single-station BOU-2017 evidence to preempt the dome's explicitly multi-station Tier 1 coherence rule — that exact concern is what the curmudgeon c5 Hole 2 identified. The author of ISS-1797 (whether the decider's digest pipeline or a curmudgeon re-pass) correctly tracked a defender retreat the dome could exploit: the rule says ≥3 stations with r>0.5, and BOU-2017 is one station. The kernel here is: 'the dome's own rule has a multi-station spatial coverage requirement that single-station temporal evidence cannot rebut, even if the temporal alignment is suggestive.' Where ISS-1797 missed is in timing — the fix had already landed 57 hours earlier under the canonical ISS-1758, and two intervening duplicate closures (ISS-1764, ISS-1808) had already established that any new filing on the same Hole 2 should be auto-closed. The reporter and the curmudgeon were attacking the same artifact from the same side; the fix was already integrated.
tldr_evidence: Section text at part4 byte offset 42985 already shows the verbatim multi-station INTERMAGNET parenthetical (207 observations, 39 eclipses including Aug 2017, ≥3-station Chapman-pattern, satisfies the dome's own multi-station threshold) — exactly the option (a) fix recommended by the c5 Hole 2 review. Fix landed via decider self-apply 2026-04-30T17:21:08Z; ISS-1797 filed 2026-05-02T01:21:53Z (57h after fix). Two prior duplicates (ISS-1764, ISS-1808) already closed already-fixed-by-source against the same canonical ISS-1758.
tldr_verdict: Close ISS-1797 as already-fixed-by-source. The multi-station INTERMAGNET parenthetical is present in data/sections.json part4 at offset 42985 and has been there since 2026-04-30T18:05Z (commit e250e427). ISS-1797 is the third duplicate of canonical ISS-1758 in the SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 Hole 2 cluster. No patch, no test, no rebuild needed.
next_steps_for_decider: Single action: close ISS-1797 with resolution_kind=already-fixed-by-source, fixed_by_iss=ISS-1758, fixed_at=2026-04-30T17:21:08Z. Fix-details: 'Decider self-apply 2026-04-30T17:21:08Z (per ISS-1758 closure) added the multi-station INTERMAGNET ensemble parenthetical to SEC-4.2.2 satisfying the dome Tier 1 ≥3-station threshold; current part4 byte offset 42985 contains the verbatim text. ISS-1797 is the third duplicate filing on the same c5 Hole 2 (after ISS-1764 closed 2026-05-01 and ISS-1808 closed 2026-04-30, both already-fixed-by-source). The Tier 1 coherence-rule retreat is closed; no further action on §4.2.2 EXP-275 paragraph required.' Optional: route a single-line attention-inbox note to tinker re: the four-duplicate-ISS pattern in the SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 review cluster, if the curmudgeon-digest dedup logic warrants investigation.

duplicate_isses.fix_details: Already fixed 2026-04-30 in decider applied patch 1 of suggested-patches-2026-04-30T17-20.json

cross_iss_cluster_audit.structural_signal_for_tinker: Four distinct ISS-NEW entries (ISS-1758, ISS-1764, ISS-1797, ISS-1808) were generated from the same SEC-4.2.2-EXP275.c5 Hole 2 finding across a ~48-hour window (2026-04-30 to 2026-05-02). The curmudgeon-digest dedup logic should ideally have collapsed these into a single ISS at digest time. The dedup gap likely stems from: (a) the c5 review file was digested multiple times if it was re-pushed or re-indexed, or (b) the digest's title-similarity check didn't catch slight wording variations across digests (compare ISS-1758 'Dome Tier 1 coherence rule requires...' vs ISS-1764 'EXP-275 new paragraph pre-empts with...' vs ISS-1797 'The EXP-275 paragraph cites BOU-2017 single-station...' — three distinct lead-ins but identical Hole 2 substance). Worth a tinker investigation if similar duplicate clusters appear elsewhere in the c-review backlog. Not a P1 — closed-issues.json already absorbs the duplicates via already-fixed-by-source, so the noise is contained.
